free_text: The source strength was 5 x 10**6 fis/sec.
Irradiations were for 1 to 17 days duration.
Sample was 3 micro-g Cf-252, deposited on a nickel
Method of Terrell (see REL-REF). An aluminum foil,
4.5 mg/cm*2 thick is placed above the Cf-252 source,
shielded from direct contact with the Cf-252 by a thin
foil of formvar, thickness equivalent to 1 mg/cm*2 of
aluminum. Fission products are collected on the
aluminum foil, and chemically separated into
alkali-earths, ruthenium and zirconium and niobium.
Individual fission products were measured by
Summation of yields for masses 125 to 157 is
normalised to 100, giving a yield for the Ba-140
Correction for daughter product yields using a
Gaussian distribution within each isobaric chain.
This correction is small except for Cd-177.
The quoted yields are not cumulative yields
for individual isotopes but are mass chain yields.
Date is corrected, reference changed - SM
SM: Nine Subents added from JRC,26,107,1975
Subent 003 deleted. All data are moved to DECAY-DATA
.Yields of Ru, Ag, Cd and Sb isotopes are given
in arbitrary units in table 4, and corrected
mass chain values are included in table 5.
SD: DECAY-DATA added, FLAG deleted, info
moved to DECAY-FLAG, a few values corrected.
SD: SF4=Sb-129 -> Sb-129g, SF5=M+ added to
SD: SF5=IND/M+ -> CUM in REACTION code.
SD: SF5=M+ added to REACTION code (IT=64%).
SD: SF5=IND/M+ -> CUM in REACTION code.
